hard.ais_why: Customer Kevin at Texas State states a strong need around AI-led efficiency, compliance automation, and better UX versus SAP and manual work. Opportunity covers eSourcing, risk, and contract, with AI-led efficiency tagged as the primary differentiator versus the incumbent SAP/manual workflow. Created late April 2026 and still in early exploration.

north_star_verdict.headline: Cold 73 days after a single informational learn-about-AI call on 24 April 2026 with Kevin, a 3-month-in assistant procurement director who liked incumbent Jaggaer and closed with 'maybe someday down the future you guys could fit into that.' No buying motion, no champion, no contact roles, no near-term sourcing event. Re-qualify against a real trigger or disqualify.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 24 April 2026; close 29 December 2026 is 176 days out from 06 July 2026. Stage Qualified since 27 April 2026 (about 70 days in stage). Single buyer call 24 April 2026, now 73 days ago; no buyer-facing activity since, the logged next step (share decks) is not confirmed executed, and zero contact roles are on the record. The December close date is a placeholder, not a buyer-validated timeline. Dark for 73 days after one exploratory call with no buying motion puts this Off Track.
evidence: Single discovery call 24 April 2026 with Kevin (Assistant Director of Procurement, Texas State University); Kevin framed it himself as 'a learning call more about how we use AI' and said 'I don't know, to be honest with you, just the informational' (24 April 2026).
Kevin is three months into the role: 'I've been here for 3 months... coming up the end of the third' (24 April 2026), and repeatedly deferred on evaluation intent because he is new.
Incumbent Jaggaer is functional and liked: 'With Jaggaer it is pretty good... we are trying to move everything into Jaggaer' (24 April 2026); Kevin has not questioned it.
No near-term sourcing event: SAP replacement is 'in the next 3 to 5 years' and that decision 'may not be our decision... a lot of what we do is wrapped up in the university system' (24 April 2026).
Closing signal was passive: 'maybe someday down the future you guys could fit into that' (24 April 2026).
Zero OpportunityContactRole records; Kevin, the sole engaged contact, is not even logged as a stakeholder.
No buyer email, call, task, or next-step execution in the 73 days since 24 April 2026; the only Salesforce activities are internal Opportunity-Health call-attempt tasks dated 27 April 2026.
The $100k Pipeline amount is unvalidated: no budget, term, approval process, or buyer commitment was discussed on the call.

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists; Kevin articulated only qualitative frustration on 24 April 2026. He described the SAP-to-Jaggaer contract-spend sync gap as producing bad decisions: 'it shows zero spend... I am making decisions based on information that is not even accurate.' He also flagged manual compliance work, saying about 8 of 11 supplier checks under Texas Senate Bill 20 are automated via PaymentWorks and 'we end up having to manually do some checks.' None of this was sized (no FTE hours, cycle time, error rate, or savings target), and Kevin himself called the session 'just informational,' not a value build. Move: re-engage to quantify the cost of the manual SB20 checks and the SAP-Jaggaer data-integrity gap and convert it into a dollar-anchored business case.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Kevin (is_rep: false)

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is unmapped and Kevin explicitly signalled the money decision sits above him: 'a lot of what we do is wrapped up in the university system... it may not be our decision... we are just one university amongst 5 or 6 other universities that are part of the Texas State University System' (24 April 2026). Kevin is an operational compliance approver ('my role is mainly a compliance role... I approve requisitions'), not a budget owner, and no OpportunityContactRole or budget-authority field names anyone. The evidence points to buying authority residing at the Texas State University System procurement/IT level rather than the campus. Move: identify and reach a Texas State University System procurement or IT leader with budget authority before treating this as a live opportunity.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Kevin (is_rep: false)

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria exist because there is no active evaluation; the call was a generic platform walkthrough, not a use-case or fit review. The only buyer-stated interests that could become criteria surfaced informally: AI-native automation ('I know AI is the future... allow it to become a tool that is used to do things much more efficient'), better user experience than SAP BP1 ('it is just more manual work... the user friendliness is not there'), and automating the residual Texas SB20 supplier-risk checks ('if that risk management had the ability... that would be a plus'). These are unconfirmed wishes, not weighted requirements. Move: run a real discovery to convert Kevin's stated AI, UX, and compliance interests into documented, weighted evaluation criteria.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Kevin (is_rep: false)

decision_process.narrative: There is no buyer decision process running; the 24 April 2026 call was a single, self-described informational meeting with no next event agreed and no evaluation initiated. Kevin outlined a hypothetical future path only: contracts consolidating into Jaggaer and an SAP replacement 'out there for RFP' in '3 to 5 years,' with the caveat that the decision 'may not be our decision' but the Texas State University System's. No approval chain, timeline, or committee was named. Move: establish whether any near-term procurement or sourcing trigger exists (a Jaggaer renewal, a contract expiry, or a system-level initiative) and map who would own that decision.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Kevin (is_rep: false)

paper_process.narrative: No paper process is in view: no MSA, order form, SOW, legal, procurement, or InfoSec activity has been discussed because the deal has not reached any commercial stage. The only contracting-relevant context is that Texas State is a public state institution governed by the Texas Comptroller ('we have to comply with all the laws of the state of Texas... a comptroller of accounts here in Texas that governs our spend'), which means any real purchase would run through a formal state solicitation ('obviously that will go out for solicitation'). Move: once qualified, map the Texas State University System public-procurement and solicitation requirements that any Zycus purchase would have to satisfy.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Kevin (is_rep: false)

identify_pain.narrative: Real but low-urgency operational pain is confirmed, owned by Kevin. First, the SAP-to-Jaggaer contract-spend sync gap corrupts his budgeting: 'SAP does not send that information back to Jaggaer... it shows zero spend... I am making decisions based on information that is not even accurate' (24 April 2026). Second, manual Texas SB20 supplier-compliance screening: roughly 3 of 11 checks are done by hand outside PaymentWorks. Third, SAP BP1 usability ('it reminds me of back in the nineties... using p-codes... you can only have up to 5 screens'). These are genuine frustrations, but Kevin voiced them as annoyances he has learned to work around, not as a funded, must-fix problem, and he owns compliance and reporting rather than the systems roadmap. Move: reframe the SB20 manual-check burden and the contract-spend data gap as a fundable business case tied to a compliance-risk or audit event.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Kevin (is_rep: false)

champion.narrative: There is no champion. Kevin is a friendly, curious operational contact who took a learning call and appreciated the walkthrough ('this is the first time I think I've gone through and looked at your system... good to go through all that'), but he has not advocated internally, driven urgency, introduced anyone, or committed to a next step. His own framing is passive ('maybe someday down the future you guys could fit into that') and he is three months into the role with no evidence of influence over the systems decision, which he indicated sits at the university-system level. Zero contact roles are logged, so the deal is single-threaded to a non-champion. Move: assess whether Kevin can be developed into a coach who opens the door to a system-level procurement or IT leader; if not, multi-thread directly to power.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Kevin (is_rep: false)

competition.narrative: The real competition is inertia around a liked incumbent, not an active bake-off. Jaggaer runs Texas State's sourcing, contracts, catalogs, and marketplace and Kevin is broadly satisfied ('with Jaggaer it is pretty good... we are trying to move everything into Jaggaer'), even noting Zycus and Jaggaer 'parallel each other very well.' SAP BP1 is the disliked ERP, but it is a 3-to-5-year replacement horizon and not a procurement-suite rival to Zycus (Kevin confirmed he understands Zycus is not an ERP). PaymentWorks is a point supplier-vetting tool, not a suite competitor. Coupa was named only by the rep as the market leader, not by the buyer as an evaluation option. No buyer-initiated evaluation of alternatives is under way, so the dominant threat is do-nothing / Jaggaer entrenchment. Wedge: AI-native automation (Merlin autonomous negotiation, contract AI redlining) and automating the residual Texas SB20 checks Jaggaer plus PaymentWorks still leave manual; but this wedge is unactivated. Move: attach the wedge to a dated trigger or accept the deal is not yet real.
sources: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Kevin (is_rep: false)

deal_movement.summary: The deal has not moved since qualification. It was created 24 April 2026 off a single inbound-style discovery call, auto-progressed Initial Interest to Qualified on 27 April 2026 and reassigned to Bailey Erazo the same day, and has had no buyer engagement in the 73 days since. The only post-call activity is a batch of internal Opportunity-Health call-attempt tasks dated 27 April 2026, none buyer-facing.

items.change: Stage auto-advanced Initial Interest to Qualified (by Noopur Handa)
Owner reassigned Abeerah Fazil to Bailey Erazo
Single informational discovery call held with Kevin; ended with no agreed next event
No buyer engagement, email, or next-step execution recorded since the discovery call

competitive_position.summary: The dominant current threat is do-nothing / Jaggaer entrenchment: Texas State runs Jaggaer for sourcing, contracts, and marketplace and Kevin is happy with it, with no active evaluation open as of 24 April 2026. SAP BP1 is a disliked ERP on a 3-to-5-year replacement horizon and is not a procurement-suite rival (medium, and only tangential to Zycus). PaymentWorks is a low-threat point tool for supplier vetting. Coupa was named only by the Zycus rep as the market leader, not by the buyer, so it is dormant here. The way in is the AI and compliance-automation wedge, but nothing has activated it.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Jaggaer entrenchment
competitors.quote: Maybe someday down the future you guys could fit into that... I don't know what I'm going to get out of this, just informational, trying to learn.
competitors.how_we_win: Manufacture urgency by tying the SB20 manual-check burden and the SAP-Jaggaer contract-spend data gap to a dated compelling event (an audit, a Jaggaer renewal, or a system-level transformation); without a clock this stays exploratory indefinitely.

competitors.name: Jaggaer
competitors.quote: With Jaggaer it is pretty good... we are trying to move everything into Jaggaer... they parallel each other very well... they are one of your biggest competitors.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus AI/Merlin (autonomous negotiation agents, contract AI redlining, supplier-risk automation for Texas SB20/boycott checks) as the next-generation upgrade over Jaggaer's less-AI-native platform, and secure a formal benchmark if Texas State opens an evaluation.

competitors.name: SAP BP1
competitors.quote: SAP reminds me of back in the nineties... using p-codes... you can only have up to 5 screens... SAP does not send contract-spend information back to Jaggaer... we are looking to replace SAP in the next 3 to 5 years.
competitors.how_we_win: SAP replacement is a 3-to-5-year horizon and a system-level decision; Zycus is not an ERP rival, so use SAP's data-sync pain as proof of the need to consolidate procurement onto a modern AI-native platform that integrates with SAP or its successor.

competitors.name: PaymentWorks
competitors.quote: PaymentWorks is the one that does all the vetting of our suppliers... about 8 of those checks are being done by them... we manually do the other 3 things.
competitors.how_we_win: Zycus Supplier Risk can absorb PaymentWorks and automate all 11 compliance checks (Texas SB20, boycott, country-of-operation) in one platform, eliminating the residual manual work Kevin flagged.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Most of the percentage comes from Coupa... it was a very large company... jumped into the American markets before we did.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Bailey Erazo (rep-named market context, not a buyer evaluation)
competitors.how_we_win: Not in this evaluation; named only by the rep as the market leader. Monitor but do not treat as an active threat unless Texas State names it.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Kevin voiced three informal expectations on 24 April 2026 (AI-native efficiency, compliance automation, and better usability than SAP), all of which Zycus demonstrated, but none is a confirmed evaluation criterion and none has been validated against a documented requirement.

items.quote: I know AI is the future... allow it to become a tool that is used to do things much more efficient.

items.criterion: Automate residual Texas SB20 / boycott supplier-compliance checks
items.quote: If that risk management had the ability... have that Zycus system do it for us, that would be a plus.

items.criterion: Better usability than SAP BP1 and accurate contract-spend data
items.quote: SAP does not send that information back to Jaggaer... I am making decisions based on information that is not even accurate.

items.deliverable: Send Kevin the presented sourcing deck plus a short overview deck for later reference
items.grounding_quote: I can shoot over this deck that I presented today... there is 20 more slides on it as well as maybe another short deck for you.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026, Bailey Erazo; SF Next_Step__c 'Bailey: Share Zycus decks/material'

items.quote: It may not be our decision... a lot of what we do is wrapped up in the university system.
We are looking to replace SAP in the next 3 to 5 years.

items.source: OpportunityContactRole (empty); Kevin not logged as a contact role

best_practice_check.summary: This is a dark, single-threaded early deal off one informational call. The win-strategy priorities are to re-qualify against a real trigger, reach the true (system-level) budget owner, and lead with the compliance wedge Kevin volunteered.
flags: Re-qualify or disqualify: convert the 24 April informational contact into a real evaluation by tying Zycus to a dated trigger (a Jaggaer renewal, contract expiry, or the SAP RFP), or omit the deal.
Multi-thread beyond Kevin to a Texas State University System procurement or IT budget owner, since Kevin said the decision may sit at the system level, not with him.
Lead with the Texas SB20 compliance-automation wedge, the one pain Kevin volunteered enthusiasm for, rather than a generic platform re-demo.

items.name: Kevin Kolb
items.title: Assistant Director of Procurement (compliance and reporting)
items.sentiment: Positive and curious, but passive; framed the engagement as informational and long-term ('maybe someday down the future you guys could fit into that').
items.risk: Single point of contact three months into the role, in an operational compliance seat with no budget authority; he flagged the decision may sit at the university-system level, so the deal is anchored to a non-champion with no access to power.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026 (named speaker); no OpportunityContactRole on record

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Kevin Kolb is an engaged, friendly operational contact who took an informational call and expressed openness, but he has not advocated internally, driven urgency, introduced anyone, or committed to a next step, and his closing remark ('maybe someday down the future') signals passive interest. Zero contact roles are logged and he indicated the systems decision is above him, so the deal is single-threaded to a non-champion.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 24 April 2026; OpportunityContactRole (empty) 06 July 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin/AI story was demonstrated generically during the 24 April walkthrough (autonomous negotiation agents, contract AI redlining, supplier-risk automation) but was never tied to Kevin's specific compliance pain with a quantified outcome, so the differentiation did not land as value. Under-positioned relative to the buyer's genuine AI curiosity.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS reads 7.0 / Moderate. The call confirms real personal AI curiosity from Kevin (he printed a ChatGPT-generated chart of AI platforms and is comparing tools) but also skepticism of generative AI and, critically, no organisational AI mandate. The AIS_why framing of an 'explicit AI mandate from the decision maker' is over-stated: Kevin is not a decision maker and the interest is individual, not an org directive.

items.detail: Economic buyer unmapped; Kevin indicated budget authority likely sits at the Texas State University System, not with him.
Do-nothing / incumbent satisfaction: Texas State is content with Jaggaer and has no active evaluation, so inertia, not a rival, is the primary threat.
No compelling event; the only named horizon is a 3-to-5-year SAP RFP and the December 2026 close date is a placeholder.
Single-threaded to one operational contact three months into the role, with the real decision residing at the multi-university system level.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal has been cold for 73 days after a single informational call with no buying motion, no champion, and no logged next-step execution. This is a dark, status-quo-anchored early deal.

Kevin volunteered clear interest in automating the roughly three manual Texas SB20 supplier-compliance checks his team still runs by hand, a concrete wedge to re-open the deal.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This is a dark, unqualified early deal: one 24 April 2026 learn-about-AI walkthrough with Kevin, a compliance-focused assistant procurement director three months into the role, who liked incumbent Jaggaer and deferred with 'maybe someday'; 73 days silent since, with no champion, no budget owner, and no near-term sourcing event.

win_position.text: We are behind the status quo, not ahead: the sole 24 April call was informational, the buyer is content with incumbent Jaggaer ('with Jaggaer it is pretty good'), and no evaluation of Zycus is open.
No budget owner is engaged and Kevin flagged the decision likely sits at the Texas State University System level, so we are single-threaded to someone without authority.
No compelling event exists; the only named horizon is a 3-to-5-year SAP RFP, and the December 2026 close is a placeholder.

Engagement is still a single generic platform walkthrough that the buyer himself called 'just informational,' with no next meeting requested.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer made no commitment: Kevin closed with 'maybe someday down the future you guys could fit into that' and named no date, next step, or internal stakeholder.
No contact roles are logged and Kevin, three months into the role, has not advocated internally or introduced anyone.

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is do-nothing / Jaggaer entrenchment: the buyer is satisfied with the incumbent and there is no severe enough pain to disrupt the status quo.
The $100k Pipeline amount and December close are speculative, with no validated size, term, budget, or approval process.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only, unquantified: SAP-Jaggaer contract-spend sync gap producing inaccurate budgets and roughly three manual Texas SB20 supplier checks (24 April 2026); no numbers, ROI, or savings target stated.
